page: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor configuration includes an L3 cache of 480 MB, alongside L1d and L2 caches of 48 KB and 2 MB respectively. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 417,805.84 and a PassMark CPU score of 181,125.91. The hardware excels in memory bandwidth, achieving 30,905.23 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 1,170.89 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in database operations, processing 510.74 million Redis SET operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 178.42 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score in the average tier.

The server exhibits distinct performance tradeoffs. While prompt processing for large language models is strong, text generation speed for a small 135M model falls into the bottom 10% tier at 35.9 tokens/sec. The absence of local storage requires external network-attached storage solutions. Given the high compute density, advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and AMX, and 100 Gbps network baseline, this server is configured for heavy multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz. It includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates with a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation speed are comparatively lower. Supported by advanced vector and matrix extensions, the hardware is designed for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-96xl is a compute-optimized bare-metal server with 384 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal server with 384 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform featuring 384 vCPUs and DDR5 memory, optimized for intensive multi-threaded computing and high-throughput networking.
bullet_points: Powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores
Equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
Delivers 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage
Achieves top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmarks
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AMX
Designed for software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving
